category_title: Year 4 student with ADHD symptoms and Literacy difficulties
minimal_context: Yiannis is expected to remain seated at his desk near the teacher during a 90-minute Literacy block.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We are starting our 90-minute Literacy block now. Please sit at your desk and open your reading book.

STUDENT: (Moves back and forth in his chair in a nervous manner) Okay.

TUTOR: Please keep reading the story.

STUDENT: (Stumps his feet and nervously moves his palms back and forth on his thighs) Are we done yet?

TUTOR: We still have a lot of time left in the Literacy block. Just stay in your seat.

STUDENT: (Flaps his arms, leaves his seat, and starts moving around the classroom) I can't sit down anymore!

options.A: Break the seating goal into sub-targets, such as remaining seated for 10 minutes the first week and increasing to the full 90-minute Literacy block the second week.
options.B: Use a storytelling cycle starting with a read-aloud using a presentation, followed by comprehension activities, and ending with online recording practices during the Literacy block.
options.C: Ask open-ended questions during the 90-minute Literacy block so that the student can write about topics he already knows and is familiar with.
options.D: Avoid using the assigned reading series and instead allow the student to self-select books from the book corner that match his reading level and interests.
